chunks.text: EECS Network in a degraded state | IRIS Skip to main content Skip to primary navigation Skip to primary sidebar EECS Network in a degraded state The EECS network is currently running in a degraded state due to an unknown issue. You may notice slownes

te due to an unknown issue. You may notice slowness or difficulty reaching some sites or services. Network staff are coming onsite and will post an update once more information is available. UPDATE [2013-12-03 10:24:21 | Derek Calderon] We currently

013-12-03 10:24:21 | Derek Calderon] We currently believe the issues are related to an upstream power issue sourced within the PG&E grid. EECS network staff are working with facilities to determine the scope and scale of the issue and its impact. Res

e scope and scale of the issue and its impact. Rest assured we are doing everything we can and hope to have the network functioning normally as soon as possible and we apologize for any inconvenience these issues have caused. UPDATE [2013-12-03 15:41

these issues have caused. UPDATE [2013-12-03 15:41:05 | Derek Calderon] We have located and resolved the source of the outage. All services should be stabilizing now. Please contact help@eecs if you notice any further issues. Again, we apologize for

otice any further issues. Again, we apologize for the problems during this busy time of the semester. Thanks, IRIS IDSG Network Group Resolved as of 2013-12-03 15:39:00 Primary Sidebar IRIS Service Status We have 0 Active Incidents, and 1 Scheduled M
atus We have 0 Active Incidents, and 1 Scheduled Maintenance noted. IST Service Status Outages to campus services are listed at berkeley.statusdashboard.com .
